seo.title: 10 Best Hotels in Mae Hong Son 2026 — Budget to Boutique
seo.metaDescription: Find the best hotels in Mae Hong Son for every budget. From $8 guesthouses on the lake to boutique resorts in teak forests. Real prices and honest reviews.

intro: Mae Hong Son is no luxury resort town — it's a small, remote mountain capital where accommodation leans budget to mid-range, with a handful of charming boutique options that make the most of the natural setting. The best places to stay cluster around Jong Kham Lake or on the outskirts amid rice paddies and mountains. Booking ahead is essential during the cool season (Nov–Feb) when the sea of mist draws Thai tourists and rooms fill fast. Here are 10 hotels and guesthouses we recommend.

hotels.name: Sang Tong Huts
hotels.description: One of Mae Hong Son's oldest and most beloved guesthouses, established in 1987. Eco-friendly bamboo and wood chalets are set in a delightful tropical garden, each tastefully decorated with traditional local materials. The owner is a wealth of information about the area and can arrange treks and tours. Simple but full of character.
highlights: Charming bamboo chalets in a tropical garden
Decades of local knowledge from the owner
Walking distance to Jong Kham Lake and morning market

hotels.name: Baan Mai Guesthouse
hotels.description: A family-run guesthouse with a cute, homey setup near Jong Kham Lake. Simple rooms with fans or air-con, friendly staff with a family-style atmosphere, and a communal area where guests mingle over breakfast. The location puts you steps from the lakeside temples and morning market.
highlights: Steps from Jong Kham Lake and temples
Friendly family atmosphere
Good travel advice from long-time owners

hotels.name: Jongkham Place
hotels.description: Cute wooden chalets in a well-tended garden, just a short walk from Jong Kham Lake. With only four rooms, it feels intimate and personal. The chalets are well-appointed for the price with hot water, air-con, and tasteful decor. Reserve well in advance — it fills up quickly during peak season.
highlights: Only 4 rooms — intimate and quiet
Wooden chalets with garden setting
Prime lakeside location

hotels.name: Baiyoke Chalet Hotel
hotels.description: A well-established mid-range hotel on the main road with rooms that have seen regular updates. Efficient and helpful staff with decent English make this a reliable choice for travelers who want a proper hotel with air-con, hot water, TV, and in-room WiFi. Popular with both local and foreign visitors.
highlights: Reliable mid-range comfort on the main road
Helpful English-speaking staff
Good base for exploring with motorbike rental nearby

hotels.name: Imperial Mae Hong Son Resort
hotels.description: Mae Hong Son's largest and most well-known hotel, set within a teak wood forest on the outskirts of town. Offers a 24-hour front desk, swimming pool, fitness center, an on-site restaurant, and free WiFi. Rooms are spacious if somewhat dated, but the forested setting and pool make up for it. Often the only option during festivals when smaller places fill up.
highlights: Outdoor swimming pool in a teak forest setting
Largest hotel in town — rooms available when others are full
On-site restaurant with Thai and international food

hotels.name: Fern Resort
hotels.description: A charming eco-resort set among rice paddies and gardens 6 km south of town. Bungalows are built from natural materials with polished wood floors, open-air bathrooms, and private verandas overlooking the countryside. The on-site restaurant serves excellent Thai food. A peaceful retreat that embodies the slow Mae Hong Son lifestyle — you'll need your own transport.
highlights: Eco-bungalows surrounded by rice paddies
Excellent on-site Thai restaurant
Peaceful countryside setting away from town

hotels.name: Phu Pai Art Resort
hotels.description: A boutique resort surrounded by rice fields and distant mountain views, offering three types of guest bungalows with pitched grass roofs, traditional decor, and modern comforts. The swimming pool is a welcome addition after a day of exploring. The on-site restaurant and bar make it self-sufficient for guests without transport.
highlights: Swimming pool with mountain views
Thatched-roof bungalows in rice field setting
On-site restaurant and bar

hotels.name: Mae Hong Son Mountain Inn & Resort
hotels.description: A comfortable mid-range resort on the edge of town with well-maintained rooms, a small garden, and mountain views from upper floors. Good value for the comfort level, with air-conditioning, hot water, and reliable WiFi. The restaurant serves solid Thai food and the staff can arrange local tours.
highlights: Mountain views from upper-floor rooms
Quiet location on the town edge
Tour arrangement service

hotels.name: Panorama Hotel
hotels.description: A modern-ish hotel in the town center offering clean, spacious rooms with air-con and hot water at reasonable prices. The rooftop area provides views toward Doi Kong Mu. Central location makes it easy to walk to the lake, temples, and morning market. One of the more recently renovated options in town.
highlights: Central location near Jong Kham Lake
Rooftop views toward Doi Kong Mu
Recently renovated rooms

hotels.name: Pang Ung Camping & Tents (Royal Forestry Department)
hotels.description: Not a hotel but a unique accommodation experience — lakeside camping at Pang Ung reservoir run by the Royal Forestry Department. Rent a tent (with mattress and bedding) right on the shore of the pine-fringed lake. Wake up to mist swirling across the water at dawn. Basic shared bathroom facilities. Bring warm clothes — it gets cold.
highlights: Lakeside tent camping at 1,600 meters elevation
Sunrise mist views directly from your tent
Bamboo rafting on the lake
